title: Vetkoek
excerpt: When my mother didn't know what to make for dinner, she baked vetkoek. There was always lovely curry mince to go with it, or you could eat it with syrup/jam and cheese. I made it for my children too. To this day it's comfort food in our house. …
html:
When my mother didn't know what to make for dinner, she baked vetkoek. There was always lovely curry mince to go with it, or you could eat it with syrup/jam and cheese. I made it for my children too. To this day it's comfort food in our house. Just drain it well once you've finished frying. Your dough should also be nice and firm. That way the vetkoek doesn't soak up too much oil.

Ingredients

  • 500 g cake flour
  • 10 g yeast
  • 10 ml salt
  • 20 ml sugar
  • 250 – 300 ml lukewarm water

Method

  1. Put the flour, yeast, salt and sugar in a bowl and mix through.
  2. Now slowly add the water and work it in with your hand until it starts to form a dough.
  3. Now you need to knead for 10 minutes if you don't have an electric mixer. The dough should be nice and firm and elastic. If you have a Kenwood you can do these steps in your machine with the dough hook. Let the machine run for 10 minutes.
  4. Grease a bowl and let the dough rise to double its size in your bowl. Cover the bowl with a cloth.
  5. Sprinkle flour on your surface and flatten the dough with your hand until it's about 2 cm thick. Now take a glass, cutter or an empty tin and press out your vetkoek.
  6. Place them on a greased baking tray, cover with a cloth and let rise again.
  7. Pour oil into a pot and let it heat up. Drop in a small piece of dough, and if it rises to the top and turns brown, your oil is ready. Now fry about 3 vetkoek at a time. Turn them over once they're nicely browned.
  8. Drain on kitchen paper. And then enjoy!
